figures.caption: Figure 2. Representative FAMS gating strategy for assessment of mitochondrial membrane 218 potential (ΔΨM). (A) Size-gated events from a representative mitochondrial sample shown on 219 FSC PMT vs. SSC axes. The size gate (0.22–2 μm) is indicated. (B) Negative control sample 220 stained with MTG only, used to establish the MTG-negative baseline and set the MTG gate 221 threshold. Events falling above the gate line (MTG+) represent fewer than 0.5% of autofluorescent 222 events and define the false-positive threshold. (C) Representative MTG-stained sample showing 223 MTG-positive mitochondria above the gate threshold. (D) JC-1 staining of an MTG-only sample 224 (no JC-1) used to establish JC-1 gating in the PE channel. MTG-positive events are gated as JC-225 1-negative (ΔΨM-negative, left gate) and JC-1-positive (ΔΨM-positive, right gate). (E) 226 Representative JC-1-stained sample showing the distribution of ΔΨM-negative (MTG+JC-1−) and 227 ΔΨM-positive (MTG+JC-1+) mitochondrial populations. (F) Size overlay of ΔΨM-positive (high 228 ΔΨM) and ΔΨM-negative (low ΔΨM) mitochondrial subpopulations shown as contour plots on 229 FSC PMT vs. SSC axes, demonstrating the size heterogeneity present within each membrane 230 potential subpopulation. Compensation was not applied to JC-1-stained samples due to the 231 emission properties of the dye. All analyses were performed using a minimum of 3 × 10⁴ events 232 per sample. 233

Figure 1. Experimental workflow and fluorescence-activated mitochondria sorting (FAMS) 204 gating strategy. Cumulus-oocyte complexes (COCs) were collected from young (2-month-old), 205 aged (12-month-old), and NMN-supplemented aged C57BL/6 female mice. Following 206 hyaluronidase treatment and mechanical separation, oocytes and cumulus granulosa cells were 207 isolated independently and subjected to cell lysis to release mitochondria into suspension. Each 208 sample was analyzed separately by FAMS. Sequential gating was applied to identify and define 209 mitochondrial subpopulations: an initial size gate (0.22–2 μm) was applied to FSC PMT vs. SSC 210 plots to isolate events within the mitochondrial size range from cellular debris (left plot); 211 MitoTracker Green FM (MTG) fluorescence in the FITC channel was then used to confirm 212

Figure 5. Summary of age- and NMN-associated changes in mtDNA copy number across 367 mitochondrial subpopulations in oocytes and cumulus granulosa cells. Directional changes in 368 mtDNA copy number are shown for each mitochondrial subpopulation identified by FAMS in 369 oocytes (upper section) and cumulus granulosa cells (lower section). Subpopulations were defined 370 on the basis of mitochondrial membrane potential (high ΔΨM and low ΔΨM) and size (small, <0.3 371 μm; large, >0.6 μm). The Effect of Aging column indicates the direction of significant change in 372 mtDNA copy number between young (2-month-old) and aged (12-month-old) individuals. The 373 Effect of NMN column indicates the direction of significant change in mtDNA copy number 374 between untreated aged and NMN-supplemented aged individuals. The NMN Restoration column 375 summarizes whether NMN supplementation restored the aged subpopulation phenotype toward 376 young levels: ✓ (green) indicates restoration to values not significantly different from young; ✗ 377 (red) indicates failure to restore; ⚠ (amber) indicates an unexpected effect in which NMN 378 produced a significant change in a subpopulation that did not differ significantly between young 379 and aged individuals, or drove values beyond young levels. Orange fill, significantly elevated; blue 380 fill, significantly reduced; gray fill, no significant change. All comparisons by Mann-Whitney U 381 two-tailed test, p<0.05. 382 383
